pattern: Validation is a strict tokenization: split on the format's delimiter, require the exact group count, and check every group against the per-format rules, returning the family name only when no rule is violated.
hints: Decide the candidate family from the delimiter present (a dot suggests IPv4, a colon suggests IPv6), then validate strictly against only that family's rules.
IPv4 needs exactly four dot-separated groups, each a decimal 0..255 with no leading zeros and no stray characters; IPv6 needs exactly eight colon-separated groups, each 1 to 4 hexadecimal digits.

Reject anything that fails the group count, an empty group, a leading zero where forbidden, an out-of-range or non-numeric IPv4 byte, or a non-hex or wrong-length IPv6 group; otherwise return the matching family.

edge_inputs.description: a valid IPv6 address with mixed case and zero groups
IPv4 group with a forbidden leading zero
IPv4 trailing dot creates an empty group
IPv6 group longer than four hex digits
IPv6 empty group from a double colon
lone zero with a plus sign is not numeric

stress.description: randomly assembled IPv4-like and IPv6-like strings, some valid, many subtly broken
stress.note: mix of near-valid IPv4, near-valid IPv6, and garbage to exercise every rejection path
